LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MAYOR GAVIN NEWSOM DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND, NO OPPOSITION FILED. THIS LAWSUIT IS AN UNLAWFUL COLLATERAL ATTACK ON THE RENT BOARD PROCEEDINGS. THE LAWSUIT IS UNCERTAIN AND STATES NO CLAIM AGAINST CCSF AND MAYOR NEWSON. PLAINTIFF DID NOT FILE A GOVERNMENT CLAIM. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECTS CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-25-2010 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. The court having reviewed the file finds no good cause or substantial justification for plaintiff's failure to comply with the court's orders and rules. Therefore, it is hereby ordered that this matter is dismissed. The court finds that a less severe sanction would not be effective due to the history of lack of compliance. Proceedings not reported. (212)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-23-2010 Continued To Oct-25-10 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Plaintiff is sanctioned $350.00, for failure to appear. The OSC is re-set to 10/25/2010 at 1:30 PM, in Department 212. Proceedings not reported. (212)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-28-2010 Set For Order To Show Cause On Aug-23-10 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

HEARING ORDERED FOR APPLICATION OF WAIVER OF COURT FEES
Off Calendar May-05-2010 Denied

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-12-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MAYOR GAVIN NEWSOM DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Wayne Kessler Snodgrass, Esq. Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) (554-4675) appearing for Defendants and Moving party; Michael Murawski, Defendant in Pro Per appearing. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained without leave to amend, no opposition filed. This lawsuit is an unlawful collateral attack on the rent board proceedings. The lawsuit is uncertain and states no claim against CCSF and Mayor Newson. Plaintiff did not file a government claim. No reasonable probability that defects can be cured by amendment. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Joinder [Ccp 389]
Off Calendar Apr-12-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: MICHAEL MURAWSKI's Motion for Order Compelling Joinder [CCP 389]. Appearances: Wayne Kessler Snodgrass, Esq. Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) (554-4675) appearing for Defendants and opposing party; Michael Murawski, Defendant in Pro Per appearing and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. Murawski has not shown he is a necessary party pursuant to CCP section 389. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
